# workspaces

The top-level tenant all runs, signals, and issues live under; this group lists the ones you're in and manages members, not the keys or channels configured inside one. Dashboard: Settings -> General / Members.

**When to use:** Seeing which workspaces you belong to, creating one, renaming the current one, or listing and removing its members.

## Commands

| Command | Invocation | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `create` | `bentolabs workspaces create --data '<json>'` | new tenant (name only); no workspace selected needed |
| `list` | `bentolabs workspaces list` | workspaces you belong to; no selection needed |
| `list-users` | `bentolabs workspaces list-users` | members of the selected workspace |
| `remove-member` | `bentolabs workspaces remove-member <user_id>` | drop a member by user-id |
| `update` | `bentolabs workspaces update --data '<json>'` | rename the selected workspace (acts on the default, not a named one) |

Confirm exact arguments with `bentolabs workspaces <command> --help`.
